player:
Ummm. Wow! How interesting! 
Could I see the piece?
npc1: {npc1_name}: Good morning, I've come to deposit a piece that has been in my family for years.

{npc1_name}: By the way, could you tell me what object it is?
You seem very young and I'm not sure you can handle something so valuable.

{npc1_name}: Young man!
You don't seem very sharp.

{npc1_name}: I think I'll keep this jewel in my family.
A visit to the Library wouldn't do you any harm.

{npc1_name}: Wow! I'm impressed by your knowledge.
I think I can trust you and deposit this magnificent object in your museum.

player:
No problem.
Now I will surely be able to identify it.
npc1:
{npc1_name}: Young man, I'm back again.
I wanted to give you another chance to deposit this object in your museum.

{npc1_name}: We'll see.
Do you know what object it is yet?

{npc1_name}: I don't see any improvement in your knowledge since last time!
I'll keep this jewel in my family.

{npc1_name}: What a great improvement!
I appreciate your effort!
I think, without a doubt, I can deposit this magnificent object in your museum.

player:
It's a great responsibility.
I must identify it.
npc1: {npc1_name}: I wanted to give you one last chance to deposit the object I own here.

{npc1_name}: I hope so.
Do you know what object it is yet?

{npc1_name}: Under no circumstances do you meet the conditions to safeguard this object!
This object will remain permanently in my family.

{npc1_name}: What a great improvement!
I appreciate your effort!
I think, without a doubt, I can deposit this magnificent object in your museum.

player: It's a fascinating period, the longest of human existence.
npc2: {npc2_name}: Hello, I'm here because I need information for a research project on Paleolithic lithic industry.
{npc2_name}: Yes. That's why I chose this specialty, although I still find it difficult to date some materials.
{npc2_name}: Could you tell me which period of Prehistory this hand axe belongs to?
{npc2_name}: Wow! I thought that you, as a curator, would help me correctly date this piece.
{npc2_name}: Although you will surely find the necessary information in the Library to document this type of object.

{npc2_name}: It's a privilege to have the help of true specialists like you.
I am truly grateful.

player: I'll be happy to help you with whatever you need.
npc2: {npc2_name}: I've returned to the museum to continue my study of materials.
{npc2_name}: By the way, have you figured out which period the hand axe we saw earlier belongs to?
{npc2_name}: Anyway. Dating lithic industry is not your strong suit.
{npc2_name}: Perfect. I see you've done your homework.

player: I think I'm making progress with my knowledge.
npc2:
{npc2_name}: For my part, I continue with my research work.
Have you made progress in dating lithic industry?
{npc2_name}: Could you tell me which period of Prehistory this hand axe belongs to?

{npc2_name}: Wow. I won't bother you further with this question.
I wouldn't want to embarrass you in front of other colleagues.
{npc2_name}: Perfect. I see you've done your homework.

player:
It seems like a magnificent initiative.
Disseminating Prehistory is essential.
npc3:
{npc3_name}: Good morning! I'm from the Local Hiking Club.
We are preparing a small exhibition on technology in Prehistory.

{npc3_name}: Of course! That's why we're here.
We want to be as faithful as possible and we have doubts about some technical aspects of that period.
{npc3_name}: Could you tell us which of these materials was not used in Prehistory?
{npc3_name}: Wow, I'm afraid that answer doesn't match other inquiries we've made.

{npc3_name}: Perhaps we'll clear up our doubts if you consult your library's holdings.
In any case, I'll come back to see if you can shed light on this matter.

{npc3_name}: Perfect! That confirms what we had read.
Now our exhibition will gain much in rigor.
Thank you very much.

player: Yes, I've consulted some publications on technology in Prehistory.
npc3: {npc3_name}: I've returned, I hope you've been able to find out something about the matter we discussed.
{npc3_name}: Glad to hear it. So, have you been able to confirm which of those materials was not used in Prehistory?

{npc3_name}: I'm sorry to say that doesn't seem to be the correct answer.
I'll come back to clear up this matter once and for all.

{npc3_name}: That's the way! I see you've gotten your act together.
This confirmation will be very helpful to us.

player: Indeed. This time I'm sure.
npc3:
{npc3_name}: Last visit to resolve our doubt.
Have you learned your lesson?

{npc3_name}: Glad to hear it.
So, have you been able to confirm which of those materials was not used in Prehistory?

{npc3_name}: I'm sorry but I think that, even we, have a clearer understanding of some basic concepts.
In any case, we won't take up any more of your time.

{npc3_name}: Finally! Thank goodness.
Now we can ensure greater certainty in the content of our exhibition.

player:
It's an exciting field.
Iberian coins are full of small details.
npc4: {npc4_name}: Good afternoon. I'm a collector and I'm especially interested in Iberian numismatics.

{npc4_name}: Yes, indeed I am.
I'm inventorying my collection and identifying the reverses is key to correct cataloging.
{npc4_name}: By the way, could you tell me what the most common design is on the reverse of Iberian coins like this unit from Kelin?

{npc4_name}: Hmm, I'm not sure that's correct.
After consulting many catalogs, I've reached a different conclusion.

{npc4_name}: You would surely benefit from reviewing some monograph specialized in Iberian minting.
I'll come back later.

{npc4_name}: Exactly! A horseman with a spear.
It's a pleasure to speak with a professional who masters this subject.

player: I've reviewed some of our holdings and I think I'm in a position to answer you.
npc4:
{npc4_name}: I've returned to continue studying the pieces in your exhibition.
Have you been able to delve into my question?
{npc4_name}: Well? What is the most typical reverse of Iberian coins?

{npc4_name}: Wow, I'm afraid your answer does not match my research.
I still trust my catalogs more.

{npc4_name}: Bravo! Now you are correct.
I'm glad you were able to confirm it.

player: This time I am absolutely certain.
npc4:
{npc4_name}: This is the last time I come to consult you.
I hope you have it clear now.
{npc4_name}: What is the most common design on the reverse of Iberian coins?

{npc4_name}: Definitely, you will have to specialize more in the field of numismatics.
I'll stick with my books.
Good day.

{npc4_name}: Magnificent! A horseman.
You can now consider yourself an expert.
Thank you for your help.

player: Hello! Great, I love it when the little ones take an interest in Prehistory.
npc5:
{npc5_name}: Hello, Mr. Curator.
We're doing a school project on the Neolithic.

{npc5_name}: Yes, and my teacher told us to look closely at the stones.
This stone is very smooth, not like others that look rougher.
{npc5_name}: Do you know which stone-working technique emerged during the Neolithic and left it so smooth?
{npc5_name}: Oh! Well, I think my teacher told us something different.
{npc5_name}: Maybe if you look in those big books you have in the Library, you'll find it.

{npc5_name}: Yes, yes, polishing! I knew it!
Thank you very much!
I'll tell the teacher how much they know in this museum.

player: Yes, I've been doing some research for you.
npc5:
{npc5_name}: I've come back with my notebook.
Have you found more information about those very smooth stones yet?
{npc5_name}: So? What is the name of that stone-working technique that emerged during the Neolithic?

{npc5_name}: Oh, no. I think you've made a mistake.
I don't remember my teacher saying anything about that.

{npc5_name}: You've got it! Great!
Now my teacher will definitely give me an A.

player: Of course I remember!
npc5:
{npc5_name}: This is the last time I can come.
I'm running out of time to do my school project.
{npc5_name}: Do you remember the name of that stone-working technique that emerged during the Neolithic?

{npc5_name}: Wow, it seems very difficult.
Don't worry, I'll explain it to you myself someday when I learn it better.
Goodbye!

{npc5_name}: Good! Polishing! That's it.
Now you really seem like a true curator.
Thank you!

player:
It's a pleasure.
The study of local history is really important as a basis for understanding more general phenomena.
npc6: {npc6_name}: Good morning. I'm the official chronicler of a nearby town and I'm documenting our most remote past.

{npc6_name}: That's right.
And I would like to delve into some aspects of the daily life of our Neolithic ancestors, 'to their very core', as they say.
{npc6_name}: Specifically, to be rigorous, could you tell me which of these elements was not used in the decoration of Neolithic pottery?

{npc6_name}: Hmm, I'm afraid your sources are not entirely precise.
That does not agree with what I have been able to read.

{npc6_name}: I advise you to consult your Library.
Even in ours we have some very interesting publications on the subject.

{npc6_name}: Exactly! I'm pleased to see that you are well documented.
It will be a pleasure to return to share knowledge.

player: Yes, I've been able to consult some documents.
npc6:
{npc6_name}: I've returned to cross-check more data.
Have you had time to review my question?

{npc6_name}: Well? Have you found the anachronistic element?
Which of these utensils was not used in the decoration of Neolithic pottery?

{npc6_name}: I'm sorry to say that you persist in error.
As a chronicler, I must be inflexible on these types of issues.

{npc6_name}: Correct! I see you've used your time well.
Take note for future consultations.

player: I am confident that it is so.
npc6:
{npc6_name}: One last consultation to clear up doubts.
I hope the answer is now definitive.

{npc6_name}: Have you found the anachronistic element yet?
Which of these utensils was not used in the decoration of Neolithic pottery?

{npc6_name}: Definitely, I will inform other colleagues that this museum is not a reliable source for certain technical inquiries.
Have a good day.

{npc6_name}: Finally! The copper spatula, indeed.
You have been able to overcome my inquisitive questions.
Thank you.

player: Ah, welcome! The specialized press is always a great ally.
npc7:
{npc7_name}: Good morning, I'm a journalist from 'Historia Viva' magazine.
I'm preparing a report on technological advances in antiquity.

{npc7_name}: Thank you.
For the article, it's crucial to highlight technological leaps.
This copper axe marks a before and after, since bronze represented a true revolution.
{npc7_name}: For context, could you tell me which technique appears in the Bronze Age?

{npc7_name}: Mmm, that information does not match the information I had.
I will have to cross-check it with other sources to avoid publishing any errors.

{npc7_name}: Perhaps I will have to find someone who knows more about ancient metallurgy.
I suggest you also get up to speed.

{npc7_name}: Smelting! Exactly.
I can already see the headline.
Perfect for my report.
You'll get a special mention.

player: I think I have it clearer now.
npc7:
{npc7_name}: I've returned to get more information on other topics,
but since I'm here, I'd like to know if you've figured out the other day's question.
{npc7_name}: What is the technique that appears in the Bronze Age?

{npc7_name}: Ummm, we still don't have a clear answer
and I can't risk publishing incorrect information.

{npc7_name}: That's it! Smelting!
Now I can confirm this information!

player: This time I am sure.
npc7: {npc7_name}: One last chance to confirm the key information. Will you finally be able to help me?
{npc7_name}: What is the metallurgical technique that emerges in the Bronze Age?

{npc7_name}: Definitely, I'll look for another specialist.
Thanks anyway.

{npc7_name}: Correct! Smelting.
I'm glad you were finally able to clarify it.
It will be very helpful.

player:
There are very interesting private collections.
Many of their pieces can have great scientific value,
although most have lost their archaeological context.
npc8:
{npc8_name}: Good afternoon.
I own a rather respectable collection of ancient pottery,
and sometimes I come to museums like yours to... compare pieces.

{npc8_name}: Well.
I consider myself a connoisseur.
For example, I have a piece that, in my opinion, is a Bronze Age pot.
But a rival... I mean, another collector, insists that it cannot be from that period.
{npc8_name}: To settle the matter, tell me: Which of these ceramic forms is not characteristic of the Bronze Age?

{npc8_name}: Ha! Just what I thought.
It seems your knowledge of ceramic forms is not very deep.

{npc8_name}: You should visit some prestigious private collections.
We reach where these public collections cannot.

{npc8_name}: Wow! I am pleasantly surprised.
Indeed, the calathus is much later.
You might be surprised to know that I also have one of those.

player: My answer will be more accurate this time, I'm sure.
npc8:
{npc8_name}: I've returned.
I've been thinking about our previous conversation and wanted to give you a second chance.

{npc8_name}: Let's see.
Which of these ceramic forms is not characteristic of the Bronze Age?

{npc8_name}: Incredible.
I don't know if you're pulling my leg.
The staff of this museum are not up to the standard of my collection.

{npc8_name}: Umm, I see you've learned your lesson.
Good for you.
You finally demonstrate some good judgment.

player: I definitely have the correct answer.
npc8:
{npc8_name}: Last visit.
I hope 'third time's a charm'.

{npc8_name}: We'll see.
Which of these ceramic forms is not characteristic of the Bronze Age?

{npc8_name}: No.
Definitely, I don't know how you do things here.
I prefer to trust my own judgment.
Good day and goodbye forever.

{npc8_name}: Correct.
The calathus.
I'm glad you finally reached the right conclusion.

player: Hello! Of course, ask whatever you need.
npc9:
{npc9_name}: Hello, good day.
Excuse us, we're from 1st year of secondary school and we have to do a project on Rome.

{npc9_name}: Great, thanks.
This pot really catches our attention because it has a super cool shine and color.
{npc9_name}: Could you tell us what this type of beautiful pottery is called?
{npc9_name}: Oh, I think I saw a different name in our textbook.

{npc9_name}: We have to come back this way, so you could look it up in one of those big catalogs you have.
Or on the internet.

{npc9_name}: Terra sigillata! That's it!
Thank you very much!
The teacher will definitely give us a ten.

player: Of course I remember! Yes, I've checked Roman pottery catalogs.
npc9:
{npc9_name}: Hello, we're back.
Do you remember us?

{npc9_name}: Yes! I knew it!
Now you remember.
Thanks for everything.

player: This time you'll have the correct name.
npc9:

{npc9_name}: Terra sigillata! Confirmed and noted!

player:
Thank you! I'm glad you like it.
Do you need help with something?
npc10:
{npc10_name}: Hello! Excuse me, this museum is wonderful!
And I am especially fascinated by the Roman era.

{npc10_name}: Oh, yes! This object seems so... advanced for its time.
I think I know what it is and I'd like to mention it on my travel blog.

{npc10_name}: This guide says it's a Roman steelyard.
But do you know what these objects were used for?

{npc10_name}: Oh, I thought it was for something else.
I saw a YouTube video that related it to something different.

{npc10_name}: Perhaps you can check it in your database.
I'm sure you'll find the correct information.
I'll come back here later.

{npc10_name}: Yes! For weighing objects! That's what I thought.
Perfect!
Now I am completely sure and I can talk about it without any problem.
Thank you very much!

player: I've been doing some research, yes.
npc10:
{npc10_name}: Hello again! I've returned to see that beautiful steelyard.

Have you found out more about its use?

{npc10_name}: Mmm, I'm not sure that's correct.

{npc10_name}: Oh, yes! I think we finally agree.
It was for weighing.
Thank you for checking and confirming it.

player: Now I have no doubt.
npc10: {npc10_name}: Last visit! I have to return to my country, although I hope you have the definitive answer for my blog readers.
{npc10_name}: Great! So, what was a steelyard used for?

{npc10_name}: I'd say your information doesn't match mine.
I have many doubts and I don't know if I'll finally write about this object on my blog.
Thanks anyway.

{npc10_name}: Excellent! 'It was used for weighing objects'.
Perfect!
My followers will love this detail.
You've been very helpful!

player: Hello. I'd like you to analyze this object to see if it needs any intervention.
npc11:
{npc11_name}: Wow!
You here!

{npc11_name}: Of course! I'll do it gladly...
But first you'll have to answer a small question.
{npc11_name}: What is the main objective of preventive conservation?

{npc11_name}: Oh, dear!
I think you're a bit lost.

{npc11_name}: I'll make you suffer a little.
I'll let you know when to come back here.
{npc11_name}: Correct! Preventive conservation does not focus on directly intervening on the object, but on protecting it by controlling its environment.

player: I've been doing some research, yes.

{npc11_name}: Correct! Preventive conservation does not focus on directly intervening on the object, but on protecting it by controlling its environment.

{npc11_name}: Hello!

I'll give you time to increase your knowledge.
{npc11_name}: Correct! Light, especially UV, can cause discoloration, fragility, and aging in organic materials such as wood, textiles, and paper.

player: Yes. I've consulted some preventive conservation manual.

player: You know you're my inspiration. Here I bring another object for you to check its state of conservation.

{npc11_name}: Hello!
Were you missing my wisdom and advice?

{npc11_name}: I think you still have gaps in restoration techniques.

player: Well. I've been looking at some publications.

{npc11_name}: I think you'll have to research something about museum environmental conditions.
{npc11_name}: You know. I'll call you when I think you know something more about this.

 A person has come to make an inquiry.
